title: silene delicatula subsp. pisidica
intro: Silene delicatula subsp. pisidica, also known as Turkish catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Turkish catchfly include the ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and coughs. Herbal preparations of Turkish catchfly include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
